description: On January 22, 2012, Croatia held a national referendum on joining the European Union. After years of post-war stabilization and economic reform, the pro-EU coalition successfully convinced the electorate that membership was the primary path to security and prosperity, resulting in a decisive 'Yes' vote.

key_events.description: Accession treaty formally signed with EU leaders in Brussels.
Mainstream political parties form a unified front to campaign for 'Yes'.
Economic data released highlighting projected growth through EU structural funds.
Final push by the Catholic Church and government leaders secures support despite low turnout fears.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling scenarios where a consensus of political elites successfully pushes a pro-integration agenda in a post-conflict or developing state.
